footer.tagline: Equity swaps for independent builders. Real paperwork. No tokens.
footer.disclaimer: builderkin facilitates introductions and document templates. We are not a law firm. Consult a solicitor or attorney before signing any agreement.

hero.headline: Where builders trade slices of what they built at 2am with Cursor.
hero.subheadline: Equity, cash, or promotion deals between independent builders. Real Delaware LLC and UK Ltd paperwork. No tokens, no smart contracts.

proof.heading: What gets swapped, and why it works
proof.narrative1Heading: Promptbase meets Notion: an equity swap that took nine days
proof.narrative1: In March 2024, two builders met on builderkin. One had a prompt library with 4,200 paying users and no distribution. The other had a Notion-based knowledge tool with a newsletter list of 11,000 but no monetisation. They listed within a week of each other. The matching algorithm surfaced them as a high-fit pair based on audience overlap and complementary revenue models. After three messages and one video call, they agreed: 3% equity in each other's Delaware LLC, documented via a standard builderkin Equity Swap Agreement. The paperwork took four days to review and sign. Eighteen months later, the prompt library builder credits the newsletter cross-promotion with 900 new paying users. The knowledge tool builder used the equity stake as leverage to raise a small angel round. Neither paid a broker. Neither gave up board seats. The deal was clean, documented, and done.
proof.narrative2Heading: A cash-plus-promo swap between a solo developer and a two-person studio
proof.narrative2: A solo developer in Edinburgh had built a browser extension for academic researchers. It had 600 active users and a 4.8-star rating but no marketing budget. A two-person studio in Toronto had a research newsletter with 22,000 subscribers and a track record of successful product launches. The studio wanted a tool to offer as a subscriber perk. The developer wanted distribution. They agreed on a hybrid deal: the studio paid $1,200 upfront via Escrow.com and committed to three dedicated newsletter features over six months. In return, the developer gave the studio a white-label licence and a 2% equity stake in the extension's parent UK Ltd company. The agreement was drafted using builderkin's standard Cash-Plus-Promo template, reviewed by both parties' solicitors, and signed within eleven days of first contact. The extension went from 600 to 3,100 active users in four months. The studio now lists the tool as a core subscriber benefit.

howItWorks.heading: How builderkin works
howItWorks.step1Body: Describe what you built, what you are offering, and what you want in return. Equity percentage, cash amount, or promotion terms. Be specific. Vague listings get ignored.
howItWorks.step2Body: The matching engine surfaces projects with complementary audiences, revenue models, and swap types. You see fit scores, not leaderboards. Filter by swap type, jurisdiction, and stage.
howItWorks.step3Body: When both parties agree, builderkin generates a jurisdiction-appropriate agreement. Delaware LLC or UK Ltd. You review it, your counterpart reviews it, and both sign. The signed document is recorded on your builderkin profile.
howItWorks.screenshotAlt: builderkin interface showing project listing form

notSection.heading: What builderkin is not
notSection.item1: Not a VC platform. If you are raising a priced round, go to AngelList. builderkin is for builders who want to stay independent.
notSection.item2: Not a marketplace for services. We do not facilitate hourly work, freelance contracts, or agency retainers.
notSection.item3: Not a social network. There are no follower counts, no likes, no engagement metrics.
notSection.item4: Not a token platform. No tokens, no smart contracts, no blockchain. Every deal is a legal document.
notSection.item5: Not for VC-track founders. If your goal is a Series A, leave. This platform is for builders who want to own what they build.

underTheHood.heading: Under the hood
underTheHood.step1Desc: Builder submits project details, swap terms, and jurisdiction preference.
underTheHood.step2Desc: Algorithm scores complementarity across audience, revenue model, and swap type.
underTheHood.step3Desc: Both parties review and sign a jurisdiction-appropriate legal agreement.
underTheHood.step4Desc: Signed swap is recorded on both profiles and added to the public swap feed.

about.heading: About builderkin
about.body1: builderkin started because the tools for trading equity between independent builders did not exist. AngelList is for VC-track startups. Freelance platforms are for services. Nothing existed for the builder who wanted to swap 3% of their SaaS for 3% of someone else's newsletter tool.
about.body2: We built the matching engine, the agreement templates, and the escrow routing. We handle Delaware LLC and UK Ltd paperwork. We do not take a cut of the deal.
about.body3: builderkin is run by a small team. We are not a VC-backed company. We charge $19 per month for the pro tier and nothing else.
about.value1: Builders should own what they build.
about.value2: Legal paperwork is not optional. It is the product.
about.value3: Matching on complementarity beats matching on popularity.
about.value4: No tokens, no smart contracts, no blockchain. Ever.
about.contactBody: Have a question or want to partner with us? We'd love to hear from you.
about.generalContactNote: Reach out anytime—we respond within 24 hours.
about.notSubheading: Builderkin connects builders directly. No middleman, no markup.
about.originPara1: Builderkin started because indie hackers and solopreneurs needed a simpler way to swap equity, assets, and services without lawyers eating the deal.
about.originPara2: We built the infrastructure—transparent terms, integrated escrow, verified profiles—so builders can focus on what matters: building.
about.originPara3: Every feature is designed around real friction points we heard from the community. We listen, we ship, we iterate.
about.originQuote: The best deals happen between people who trust each other. We just make that easier.
